api/v3/handlers.purpose: Structural umbrella for all v3 HTTP handler sub-packages; each child owns one API resource slice and bridges generated api/v3 request/response types to domain services via the httptransport.Handler pattern. Every handler in this tree must follow the same decode/operate/encode contract and error-encoder chain.

patterns.name: Type-alias triad per operation
patterns.description: Each operation file declares Request, Response, and optionally Params type aliases from api/v3 generated types, keeping operation files self-contained.
patterns.example: type ListMetersRequest = api.ListMetersRequestObject; type ListMetersResponse = api.ListMetersResponseObject

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs for path-param endpoints
patterns.description: Endpoints that take URL path parameters (e.g. meterID, planID) must use NewHandlerWithArgs; parameter-less list endpoints use NewHandler.

patterns.name: Namespace resolved in decoder, never in operation
patterns.description: Every decoder closure must call h.resolveNamespace(ctx) (or equivalent injected resolver) to obtain the namespace string before building the domain input.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return req, err }

patterns.name: apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder always last in error chain
patterns.description: Every handler's AppendOptions block must include httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder()) so domain errors map to correct HTTP status codes.

patterns.name: Domain-specific errorEncoder chained before GenericErrorEncoder
patterns.description: Packages with domain-specific errors (billing, features) define a local errorEncoder() and chain it before GenericErrorEncoder so billing.NotFoundError → 404 etc.

patterns.name: convert.go owns all domain↔API mappings; convert.gen.go is generated
patterns.description: Hand-written bidirectional conversion lives in convert.go; Goverter-driven generation targets convert.gen.go. Never edit convert.gen.go directly.

patterns.name: IgnoreNonCriticalIssues=true on mutating inputs
patterns.description: Create and Update decoders must set req.IgnoreNonCriticalIssues = true so plans/addons with only non-critical validation issues are not rejected.
patterns.example: input.IgnoreNonCriticalIssues = true // set in decoder, not in domain service

key_file_guides.role: Declares the Handler interface and unexported handler struct; constructs httptransport.Handler instances and wires them to domain service fields. var _ Handler = (*handler)(nil) compile-time check is required.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Missing var _ Handler check; storing domain service under a wrong field type; omitting error encoder from any operation's AppendOptions.

key_file_guides.role: Owns all bidirectional API↔domain struct transformations for the resource. Discriminated unions (price types, app types) are hand-coded here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new union variant in TypeSpec without adding matching cases here; partial round-trips (ToAPI without FromAPI).

key_file_guides.role: Goverter-generated conversion code. DO NOT EDIT. Regenerate with make generate.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any manual edit will be overwritten on next make generate.

key_file_guides.role: Present in packages with domain-specific error types (billing, features). Maps domain error structs to HTTP status codes before GenericErrorEncoder runs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Packages that add a new domain error type without updating this file will silently return 500.

key_file_guides.role: Sub-package that builds streaming.QueryParams from MeterQueryRequest fields; shared by meters and featurecost handlers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never duplicate this logic inline; always call query.BuildQueryParams.

key_file_guides.role: Rich bidirectional transformer for the Plan → Phase → RateCard → Price hierarchy. Price type switch must be exhaustive with an unsupported-type guard.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new price type in TypeSpec without updating both ToAPIBillingPrice and FromAPIBillingPrice plus round-trip tests in convert_test.go.

key_file_guides.role: Entry point for customer billing-override sub-resource; wired separately from core customer CRUD.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Mixing billing-override operations with core customer operations in the parent package.

key_file_guides.role: Polymorphic app mapping; each app type requires its own case in the ToAPIBillingApp switch via type assertion on app.App.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new app type without a matching case causes a runtime panic or silent nil return.

anti_patterns: Using httptransport.NewHandler instead of NewHandlerWithArgs for any endpoint that takes a URL path parameter.
Resolving the namespace inside the operation closure instead of the decoder closure — breaks testability and violates the decode/operate separation.
Hand-editing any convert.gen.go file — it is always overwritten by make generate.
Omitting apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder() from an operation's AppendOptions — domain errors will return 500 to callers.
Adding business logic (validation, defaults, service calls) inside the encoder closure (third argument) — all domain calls belong in the operation closure.

decisions.decision: Each resource lives in its own sub-package under api/v3/handlers/ rather than a single flat handlers package.
decisions.rationale: Isolates generated type aliases, conversion logic, and error encoders per resource; prevents import cycles and keeps each package small and independently regenerable.

decisions.decision: Goverter is used only for struct-level field mappings; discriminated unions (price types, app types, app type strings) are always hand-coded in convert.go.
decisions.rationale: Goverter cannot express type-assertion switches or one-of union dispatch; hand-coding these makes the exhaustiveness check explicit and reviewable.

decisions.decision: Domain-specific error encoders (billingprofiles, features) chain before GenericErrorEncoder rather than replacing it.
decisions.rationale: GenericErrorEncoder handles the common model error types (NotFound, Validation, Conflict); domain encoders only need to add cases for errors GenericErrorEncoder does not know about.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new path-param v3 handler operation (e.g. GetAddon)
